title: SENIOR HEALTH INSURANCE BENEFITS ASSISTANCE - 211info
text: CENTRAL OFFICE SENIOR HEALTH INSURANCE BENEFITS ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides trained volunteers who educate, assist, and serve as advocates for people with Medicare. SHIBA volunteers help people with Medicare understand their rights and options for health insurance so that they can make informed decisions. SHIBA volunteers can also provide information on the Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB) program, a Medicaid program that helps people with Medicare pay for their medical care. 209 SW 4th Avenue Suite 510 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Individuals with Medicare and their family members or caregivers Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 988-3646 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 988-3646 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Multnomah County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Services: Health Insurance Claims Assistance for Beneficiaries Medicare Information/Counseling Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - AREA AGENCY ON AGING HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - AGING AND DISABILITY RESOURCE CONNECTION HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - OREGON MEDICARE SAVINGS CONNECT HOUSING/SHELTER - ADULT CARE OPTIONS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OPTIONS COUNSELING INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE – MEDICAID INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - PUBLIC GUARDIAN AND CONSERVATOR PROGRAM INFORMATION SERVICES - FAMILY CAREGIVER SUPPORT PROGRAM INFORMATION SERVICES - HOARDING RESOURCES PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - SNAP, MEDICAID, AND TRANSPORTATION SERVICES If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. CENTRAL OFFICE
